The AiFeedbackButtons in streaming mode receive an empty-string suggestionId. This means feedback submissions will fail server-side (no valid suggestion ID). A follow-up step should either hide feedback buttons in streaming mode or wire up the suggestionId once the backend returns it.

confidenceTier is computed from the raw `matches` list (pre-rerank), not from the reranked results. This means a query that reranks down from 10 high-score matches to 3 low-score ones will still report HIGH confidence. This is technically correct per spec but could be misleading in edge cases — worth revisiting in a future step if product feedback indicates over-reporting of confidence.

Tests don't stub `calibrationService.getCalibration()`, so Mockito will return null by default. The service gracefully handles this (catches exception, skips calibration), but tests could be more explicit about the two paths (with vs without calibration).
